cross_scene_arcs.title: From Disillusionment to Defiance: Breaking with Military Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Archie Gates' growing disillusionment with the military hierarchy culminates in open insubordination, as he confronts General Horn twice—first in a moment of personal rage, then in a public act of symbolic rejection—marking his complete moral and institutional break from the chain of command.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Archie feels morally alienated from the military's reward system and engages in escalating defiance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Archie publicly humiliates General Horn again, cementing his status as a rogue figure outside military legitimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term consequences of his repeated insubordination remain uncertain, including potential court-martial or exile.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Gold as Obsession: A Shifting Personal Mission
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Archie's initial fixation on Saddam's gold as an escape from military futility evolves into a driving mission objective, persisting through setbacks and logistical challenges, ultimately becoming the central focus that binds his leadership to both personal ambition and group survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Archie fixates on the 700 million in gold as a means to transcend the meaninglessness of war.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Archie formalizes the gold recovery as a condition for the survival of Imam's people, merging personal goal with moral obligation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The physical and ethical feasibility of extracting and profiting from the gold remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Reckoning: From Complicity to Ethical Leadership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Archie grapples with complicity in violence and abandonment, evolving from passive witness to active moral agent—refusing to profit at the cost of guilt, confronting team dissent, and ultimately choosing selfless leadership by leading refugees across the border on foot.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Archie allows the execution of a raped woman but intervenes selectively, revealing moral compromise under pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Archie abandons vehicle transport to ensure refugee safety, fully embracing selfless leadership despite personal risk.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether his late moral interventions can redeem earlier failures remains emotionally and ethically unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Leadership Transformed: From Officer to Revolutionary Guide
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Archie transitions from a conventional military officer to an ideologically driven leader, using his combat experience to justify a new code of 'necessary' violence, only to find his authority undermined by local realities and the limits of American idealism in foreign conflict.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Archie teaches brutal Gulf War tactics, positioning himself as an experienced, authoritative figure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Archie's attempt to inspire Iraqi fighters with revolutionary rhetoric fails, exposing the fragility of his assumed leadership role.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legitimacy of Archie's leadership—whether rooted in rank, morality, or force—remains contested and unstable.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Burden of Command: Loyalty, Loss, and Fractured Brotherhood
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Archie's relationship with his team deteriorates under the weight of moral decisions, particularly the abandonment of Barlow, leading to emotional confrontation with Vig and a fragile reassertion of command through shared trauma and survival necessity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Archie refuses to abandon responsibility for Barlow, emotionally overwhelming Vig with his moral stance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite growing fractures, Archie maintains command during a high-stakes escape, relying on loyalty forged through shared danger.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional rift between Archie and his team, especially over Barlow's fate, remains unhealed and threatens cohesion.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Anchor Under Fire
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Doc consistently upholds ethical standards, confronting racism and advocating for moral conduct, even as the mission deteriorates into moral ambiguity. His role as the unit's conscience evolves from enforcing discipline to making principled life choices post-mission.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Doc reprimands racial slurs and aligns with Troy to uphold unit integrity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Doc rejects illicit gain and chooses a lawful future with Archie, affirming his commitment to ethical survival.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The fragility of moral authority in a corrupt system remains, as racial tensions persist despite Doc's interventions.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Complicity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Doc transitions from disciplined soldier to active participant in a rogue mission, grappling with internal conflict between personal greed and loyalty. His initial looting marks the start of a moral unraveling that culminates in full operational complicity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Doc secretly takes jewelry, challenging mission discipline and Archie's authority.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Doc arms explosives and prepares to execute a high-risk escape, fully embedded in the illegal operation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The tension between individual desire and collective purpose remains volatile, threatening group cohesion under pressure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Compassion to Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Doc's humanitarian instincts are tested and ultimately fractured by the brutality of war. He begins as a selfless caregiver but is forced to suppress empathy for survival, culminating in a psychological breakdown that exposes his emotional limits.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Doc sacrifices his gas mask to save a civilian, embodying selfless compassion.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Doc pushes away a wounded child and later wets his pants in terror, revealing the erosion of his emotional resilience.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His ability to reconcile compassion with survival remains in question, especially as he discovers more civilians in need.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reconciliation and Renewal
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After escalating racial conflict with Vig, Doc achieves personal reconciliation, reaffirming his identity and commitment to the group. This mends a key relationship and restores his sense of purpose.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Doc confronts Vig's racial stereotypes, causing group tension and silencing by Archie.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Doc accepts Vig's apology and declares 'I'm in,' recommitting to the mission with renewed moral clarity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The depth of Vig's change is uncertain, and broader racial dynamics in the unit remain unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Burden of Leadership and Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Doc evolves from a disillusioned observer to a critical actor in the team's survival, providing medical care under fire and ultimately taking offensive action. His journey reflects a transformation from moral hesitation to decisive, life-saving agency.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Doc collapses emotionally, questioning the war's purpose and his role in it.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Doc throws the explosive football, destroys the helicopter, and takes up arms, becoming a key force in securing the team's survival.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The psychological toll of his actions and the long-term impact on his identity as a healer remain unprocessed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Weight of First Blood
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Troy's initial encounter with lethal force in the desert sets off a chain of moral and psychological conflict, as he grapples with ambiguous rules of engagement and the emotional toll of taking a life without clear justification.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Troy seeks confirmation before using force, showing hesitation and a desire to follow protocol.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Troy becomes emotionally detached, numbed to violence, and exhibits early signs of trauma, signaling a shift from moral uncertainty to psychological dissociation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The lack of accountability and support leaves Troy isolated, with no resolution to his internal conflict over the legitimacy of his actions.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Greed to Discipline
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Troy evolves from pursuing personal gain through looting to rejecting materialism entirely, culminating in the symbolic destruction of the suitcases and full alignment with the group's ethical mission.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Troy is tempted by jewelry and acts on personal desire, momentarily abandoning mission discipline.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Troy destroys the suitcases, rejecting personal profit and embracing the group's moral purpose.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The psychological burden of his earlier complicity in corruption may persist despite his transformation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Captivity and Ideological Resistance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: During prolonged interrogation and torture, Troy clings to his belief in the necessity of military intervention, engaging in a philosophical battle with his captor that tests his ideological resolve against systemic cynicism.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Troy seeks moral validation, insisting that individual actions in war still matter.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite physical humiliation and forced silence, Troy reaffirms his belief in intervention as a stabilizing force.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The broader question of whether such interventions are morally justified remains contested, with no resolution between opposing worldviews.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Path to Agency and Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Troy transitions from a broken captive to an active participant in his own rescue, overcoming paralyzing fear to execute a critical role in the escape plan.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Troy is physically and psychologically broken, surviving only through endurance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Troy lights the fuse, demonstrating courage and reclaiming agency in the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His ability to sustain this resolve under continued pressure remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mortality and Meaning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Near death from a chest wound, Troy confronts his mortality, reevaluates his past actions—especially his commodification of war—and struggles to maintain hope as his body deteriorates despite medical interventions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Troy reflects on the hollowness of profiting from war imagery as he faces death.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Though stabilized medically, Troy remains physically fragile and emotionally burdened, questioning whether he can endure the journey.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His ultimate survival and reconciliation with guilt and legacy remain uncertain.
